title: Heikin Ashi Signal EA
subtitle: Heikin Ashi Smoothed Candles + RSI Confluence — Reversal Detection with 77%+ Buy Accuracy
author: Christian Benjamin (lynnchris)
verdict: EXTRACT — Heikin Ashi formulas, shadow-to-body ratio reversal detection, and dual confirmation pattern. RSI thresholds show the value of tuned (not standard 30/70) levels.

series_evolution.part_7: Multi-TF BB + Stochastic confluence, K<5/K>95 extreme thresholds
series_evolution.part_11: Adds Heikin Ashi smoothing BEFORE applying indicator analysis. The key advance: smooth the candles first, THEN look for reversal patterns in the smoothed data. This reduces false reversals from raw price noise.

core_concept.name: Noise-Smoothed Reversal Detection
core_concept.description: Heikin Ashi smooths raw OHLC data by averaging it, removing tick noise and making trend direction visually clearer. A reversal pattern on HA candles is more reliable than on raw candles because false spikes are absorbed by the averaging. Confirm the HA reversal with RSI for a two-layer filter.
core_concept.ha_vs_raw: Traditional candles: every spike visible, many false reversals. Heikin Ashi: averaged, fewer but higher-quality reversal signals.
buy_conditions: ConsecutiveCandles of bearish HA candles detected (trend to reverse FROM)
Reversal candle has long lower shadow: shadow/body >= ShadowToBodyRatio (1.5)
sell_conditions: ConsecutiveCandles of bullish HA candles detected (trend to reverse FROM)
Reversal candle has long upper shadow: shadow/body >= ShadowToBodyRatio (1.5)
core_concept.relevance_to_creviaderiv: The HA averaging is conceptually similar to our EMA3 smoothing on M1. Both reduce noise to see clean momentum direction. The shadow-to-body ratio maps to our departure candle check — we require a candle that clearly moves AWAY from a zone, not just touches it.

heikin_ashi_formulas.note: HA candles do NOT reflect actual tradable prices. They are smoothed visual representations. SL/TP must be calculated from raw prices, not HA prices.

trend_candles.description: How many historical HA candles to analyse for trend direction

consecutive_candles.description: Minimum consecutive same-direction candles before looking for reversal

shadow_to_body_ratio.description: Minimum shadow/body ratio on reversal candle (strength filter)

rsi_buy_threshold.description: RSI must be BELOW this for buy signal (NOT standard 30)

rsi_sell_threshold.description: RSI must be ABOVE this for sell signal (NOT standard 70)

steps.name: compute_ha_candles
steps.description: Transform raw OHLC to Heikin Ashi series

steps.name: detect_prior_trend
steps.description: Check TrendCandles bars to verify a clear prior trend (all bullish or all bearish HA candles)
steps.bullish_trend: all ha_close[i] > ha_close[i+1] for i in last TrendCandles
steps.bearish_trend: all ha_close[i] < ha_close[i+1] for i in last TrendCandles

steps.name: detect_reversal_candle
steps.description: On the PREVIOUS bar, check shadow-to-body ratio in the reversal direction
steps.buy_reversal: lower_shadow = ha_open[1] - ha_low[1];  body = abs(ha_close[1]-ha_open[1]);  ratio = lower_shadow/body >= 1.5
steps.sell_reversal: upper_shadow = ha_high[1] - ha_open[1]; body = abs(ha_close[1]-ha_open[1]); ratio = upper_shadow/body >= 1.5

steps.name: confirm_direction_change
steps.description: Current bar (index 0) must close in the NEW direction
steps.buy_confirm: ha_close[0] > ha_open[0]  (bullish current bar after bearish trend)
steps.sell_confirm: ha_close[0] < ha_open[0]  (bearish current bar after bullish trend)

steps.name: rsi_confirmation
steps.description: Final RSI gate — momentum must confirm the reversal direction

steps.name: draw_signal_arrow
steps.description: If all gates pass: draw coloured arrow on chart

backtested_accuracy.note: Buy accuracy is consistently higher than sell. Asymmetry suggests the instrument bias (synthetic indices tend to trend bullish). Also: small sample size — 27 days is not statistically significant.

components_to_build.name: HeikinAshiTransformer
components_to_build.contract: heikin_ashi(opens, highs, lows, closes) -> {ha_open, ha_high, ha_low, ha_close}
components_to_build.note: Pure function. Returns all 4 HA series. First bar special case: ha_open = (open+close)/2.

components_to_build.name: ReversalDetector
components_to_build.note: Three-phase check: prior trend → shadow ratio → current bar direction.

keep: Heikin Ashi formulas — correctly defined, industry-standard implementation
Shadow-to-body ratio reversal check — structural strength filter, maps to our departure candle
Three-phase signal: prior trend → reversal candle → direction change confirmation
Tuned RSI thresholds (34/65 not 30/70) — reduces false signals vs standard levels
Asymmetric RSI (34 for buy, 65 for sell) — allows for instrument-specific tuning
discard: HA prices for SL/TP — must use raw prices for actual risk calculations
Signal-only output — needs SL/TP for production use
27-day backtest — too short for statistical significance
MQL5 chart arrows — replace with our LevelDrawer/ChartRenderer
improve: Apply HA smoothing to OUR M1 entry candles as a pre-filter — run HA on M1 before checking EMA/ADX
Add ATR-based SL from raw prices (not HA prices) once reversal detected
Add zone alignment: reversal signal at a demand zone = high-quality setup
Extend backtest to 3+ months minimum for statistical validity
Add separation by session (London vs NY) — reversal accuracy varies by session
Test shadow_ratio parameter: 1.5 is default — may be over/underfit to tested instruments

creviaderiv_insights.ha_as_pre_filter: We could apply HA smoothing to M1 bars before running our EMA3 crossover check. HA-EMA3 would be cleaner than raw-EMA3 — absorbs tick noise before the crossover signal fires.
creviaderiv_insights.shadow_to_body_maps_to_departure: The shadow/body >= 1.5 check is conceptually our departure candle gate: we require close AWAY from zone, body > X% of candle. Both ensure the reversal has structural commitment, not just a touch.
creviaderiv_insights.rsi_tuning_lesson: Standard RSI 30/70 generates too many false signals. The author used 34/65 after testing — showing that indicator thresholds should be FITTED to the instruments, not used at textbook defaults. Our ADX > 20 threshold follows the same principle.
creviaderiv_insights.buy_sell_asymmetry: 77% buy vs 60% sell accuracy is expected on synthetic indices (Boom/Crash are directionally biased). Our system should maintain separate statistics for buy vs sell signals to detect this asymmetry.

article_figures.title: Smoothened Chart Comparison
article_figures.insight: Side-by-side of raw candles vs Heikin Ashi. HA clearly shows trend direction without noise spikes.

article_figures.title: OHLC to HA Transformation Flowchart
article_figures.insight: Shows the 4-formula transformation. Confirms ha_close = avg(O+H+L+C) and sequential ha_open dependency.

article_figures.title: Backtesting Results V100
article_figures.insight: Animated backtest on Volatility 100 Index. Shows signal arrows and tracking of accuracy. 27-day window.

article_figures.title: Live Market Test V25
article_figures.insight: Live signal on Volatility 25 Index. Shows buy signal near reversal zone with RSI confirmation.
